name: Amr ibn Jundab
title: Self-Sacrificing Defender
shortDescription: A brave Ansar of Imam Hussain (AS) who fought manfully in the valiant defense of the Ahl al-Bayt on Ashura and was martyred.
fullStory:
Amr ibn Jundab al-Hadhrami was a sincere and devoted Shia who reached Karbala to help Imam Hussain (AS). His name is mentioned in authentic Ziyarat texts as one of the exalted martyrs.

When the siege tightened around the Imam's companions on the day of Ashura, Amr ibn Jundab, along with other brave Ansar, stood single-handedly against the enemy's assault so that no harm would come to the Ahl al-Bayt's tents.

With firm faith and overflowing love for the Prophet's (PBUH) son, he drew his sword and entered the battlefield. Amr attacked the Kufan ranks with Haidari bravery, striking down enemies with every blow of his sword.

After a fierce and exhausting battle, Amr ibn Jundab, whose body was a target of arrows and spears, fell to the ground in the path of defending the truth and became immortal by drinking the nectar of martyrdom.
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Amr ibn Jundab.
